changelog:
Unify the API key system — fix the duplicate UI introduced in A7.

Before A12 there were two parallel API-key systems: the legacy single-key field on the Settings tab and the multi-key list on the new API Keys tab. Same auth path validated against both. Confusing.

A12 makes the API Keys tab the single source of truth:
- Auto-migrates the legacy single cfg.ApiKey into cfg.APIKeys[0] (named 'legacy') on first load. Idempotent — only triggers when ApiKey is set AND APIKeys is empty.
- Settings tab API card no longer shows the single-key text input + Generate button; replaced with an explainer plus an 'Open API Keys tab' shortcut button.
- 'Enable API Key validation' toggle stays on Settings (it's a global on/off, not key-specific).
- The /admin/api/settings.apiKey field still exists for backward compat (it now mirrors APIKeys[0] when present), so older clients that read it keep working.

No data loss — the migration only adds, never removes.

A11: WebSocket transport for /v1/responses (Codex experimental).
A10: dashboard API Keys + Analytics tabs.
A9: entrypoint chown + su-exec hotfix.
A8: dashboard credits use account-true quotaUsed.
A7: backend multi API keys.
A6: security + reliability hardening.
